synthesis.honest_eval_protocol: Run the SAME leakage-free pseudo-prospective back-analysis (run_back_analysis / global_backanalysis) that scores base tiled ETAS, with E12 as models['primary'] and base tiled ETAS (V0) as the explicit baseline, at horizons {1,2,7} d and thresholds {5.0,6.0,7.0}, daily issue cadence, weekly refit+restack. Headline metric: per-horizon mean IGPE of E12 vs base tiled ETAS in nats, recorded as a NEW igpe_vs_base_tiled channel parallel to the existing igpe_vs_etas, computed by csep.information_gain_per_earthquake(lam_ens, lam_base, omega) and tested by the same Rhoades-2011 paired T-test + Wilcoxon W-test already in comparison_tests. Run the multi-region E5 panel (high-seismicity JP/CL/US-CA/NZ + low-seismicity US-CE/EU-W/AU-E + GLOBAL) so any gain is shown to generalize, not cherry-picked on one sequence. Also report N/M/S/L consistency (E12 must stay calibrated, not just win the comparison) and the learned weight trajectory over time (expected V0-dominant with small regime-correlated mass on V1/V2) as an auditable artifact. The evaluation period is DISJOINT from any development period used to fix rho/N_min/variant hyperparameters. The 7-day prospective number is the gate (the ContextTPP 30d->7d non-generalization is the cautionary precedent — never let a single-window 30d win stand in for the 7d prospective result). A negative or non-significant result is REPORTED as E12 in experiments.md exactly as E9 was — no quiet shelving.
synthesis.ship_decision_rule: PRE-REGISTERED in docs/experiments.md as the E12 stub BEFORE the run. E12 SHIPS (replaces base tiled ETAS as primary) iff ALL hold: (a) on the GLOBAL view AND in >=2 of the high-seismicity regional views, the mean paired IGPE(E12 vs base tiled ETAS) > 0; (b) the Rhoades paired-T 95% CI excludes zero on the POSITIVE side (t_ci_low > 0) at the 7-day horizon; (c) the W-test corroborates (w_pvalue < 0.05, positive median); (d) STABILITY: per-window IGPE is positive in >= 60% of weekly windows AND the sign does not flip on the most-recent held-out quintile (temporal-stratification guard); (e) E12 does NOT lose in any low-seismicity view by more than the noise floor (mean IGPE >= -0.005, i.e. it collapses to base where there is no triggering); (f) the negative-control shuffled-label fit does NOT score as well as the real fit (kill-switch clear); (g) E12 passes N/M/S/L consistency no worse than base. The GLOBAL-plus->=2-regions requirement (not any-single-view) controls multiple-comparison region-shopping across the 8 views. If ANY of (a)-(g) fails, E12 is RECORDED AS A DEAD-END with the measured (likely ~0 or noise) IGPE — 'no demonstrated prospective gain over single tiled ETAS' is itself a valid, publishable result, exactly as E9 was recorded.
synthesis.expected_outcome: Given the adversarially-verified prior (+0.016+/-0.028 IGPE, Herrmann & Marzocchi 2023; the CI already straddles zero), the honest base-rate expectation is a SMALL positive global IGPE on the order of +0.01 to +0.02 nats, QUITE POSSIBLY not statistically significant at our M>=5 event count (few independent target windows). The three members are all tiled ETAS on the same tiles, so their rate fields are highly correlated (>0.9) and the log-score surface is nearly flat in w — capping the upside hard and frequently driving the safe outcome w≈e_0 (E12 ≈ base, no gain, no harm). A LARGE jump would itself be a red flag for leakage and should trigger an audit, not celebration. Most likely E12 records as 'positive but not significant' => a pre-registered NO-SHIP and an honest dead-end entry in experiments.md (the same disposition as E9), with the structural guarantee that E12 never does WORSE than base by more than weight-estimation noise. The value delivered is a correctly-built, leakage-free, honestly-evaluated stacking pipeline that settles whether the only literature-proven prospective lever is real for OUR global M>=5 catalog — and the reusable fit_weights_from_history + igpe_vs_base_tiled machinery regardless of the verdict.
